KING RONALDO

Twenty-four years since his senior debut, Cristiano Ronaldo is still chasing a number no footballer has ever officially reached. This is the chase, the trophies, and the empire built alongside it.

The Nickname

G.O.A.T

Greatest Of All Time — earned across five clubs, five Ballon d'Ors, and a trophy cabinet that took two decades to fill.

1985 — 2026

Five Clubs, One Shirt Number

Born in Funchal, Madeira, on 5 February 1985. Every era, in order.

02
Sporting CP

The boyhood club

Signed as a teenager and made his senior debut for his hometown federation's flagship club in 2002 — the launchpad that got Manchester United's scouts on a plane to Lisbon.

03
Man United

Sir Alex signs a teenager

A £12.24m move to Old Trafford in 2003 and the No.7 shirt once worn by Best, Robson, and Beckham. Six years, three Premier Leagues, and a Champions League followed.

08
First Ballon d'Or

Treble year

Premier League title, Champions League trophy, and his first Ballon d'Or in the same season — proof the United academy product had become the best forward in Europe.

09
Real Madrid

A world-record galáctico

Moved to the Bernabéu for a then-world-record fee. Over nine seasons he became the club's all-time leading scorer with 450 goals in 438 appearances and won four Champions Leagues.

16
Portugal

Euro 2016 champion

Captained Portugal to their first-ever major international trophy, limping off in the final and coaching from the touchline as his teammates finished the job.

18
Juventus

Chapter three: Turin

Left Madrid as its record scorer to chase a new challenge in Serie A, adding two league titles and an overhead kick that got a standing ovation from the away end.

21
Man United

The return

Back at Old Trafford eighteen years after he first arrived — still scoring on his second debut, still wearing No.7.

23
Al-Nassr

Riyadh, and a new record

Joined the Saudi Pro League and became the first player in history to score 100 goals for four different clubs, dragging the league's global profile up with him.

26
World Cup, 6th time

Past Eusébio

At 41, became the first player ever to score in six different FIFA World Cups, and moved past Eusébio as Portugal's all-time top scorer at the tournament — still chasing the trophy that has eluded him.

Off The Pitch

Building An Empire in Funchal's Name

The businesses, the family, and the brand that outgrew the sport.

CR7
Net worth$1.2B+
Forbes Billionaires List2026 debut
Social following1B+
Most-followed, Instagram#1
Hotel cities6+
Fragrance lineCR7
Airport named in his honorMadeira
Hospitality

Pestana CR7 Hotels

A 50/50 partnership with Portugal's Pestana Group, launched in his hometown of Funchal in 2016 and now spanning Lisbon, Madrid, Marrakech, and New York, with Paris and Manchester next.

Heritage

Museu CR7, Funchal

A museum on Madeira's harbourfront holding his trophies and individual awards — right next door to the hotel that shares his initials.

Investment

CR7 Sports Investments

Took a 25% ownership stake in Spanish club UD Almería in February 2026 and co-owns Footlab, a chain of AI-powered football training facilities.

Family

Georgina & the kids

His life off the pitch with partner Georgina Rodríguez and their children is as closely followed as anything he does on it — a fixture of the same brand that sells hotel rooms and fragrance.
